articles.title: FOUNDATIONS FOR VALUE MONETIZATION
articles.summary: AI companies competing today need a system of capabilities that can enable them to deliver and capture value through pricing, packaging, and billing. And with the AI transforming work across the market, monetization needs a version update.

articles.title: YOUR NEXT JOB WILL REQUIRE AI SKILLS
articles.summary: AI fluency has become a key hiring factor, with roles requiring skills that range from basic tool use to advanced workflow automation. This post presents a six-step framework that helps evaluate curiosity, creativity, problem-solving, and the ability to apply AI in real-world scenarios.

articles.title: WHY PRODUCT PEOPLE CAN'T COAST THROUGH THE AI TRANSITION
articles.summary: Most GenAI pilots fail to deliver returns, but success comes from small, practical automations. Product professionals who bridge AI capabilities with business realities will be vital in turning experiments into measurable results.

articles.title: PRODUCT LEADERSHIP ARCHETYPES
articles.summary: Product leaders fall into three archetypes: craftsperson, operator, and visionary. The most effective leaders combine deep product craft with coaching to scale teams, rather than relying on process alone.

articles.title: MINIMALLY VIABLE CONSISTENCY
articles.summary: Minimally viable consistency means using only the essential shared language and structures needed to coordinate effectively, balancing clarity with flexibility based on organizational context.

articles.title: TURN JOB POSTINGS INTO A PRODUCT FEATURE YOUR CUSTOMERS WILL LOVE
articles.summary: From MARKET-SCALE TRENDS TO TEAM-LEVEL HIRING SIGNALS, it's structured, AI-ready data so your engineers can focus on building features instead of wrangling data.

articles.title: KEEPING YOURSELF MOVING AS A PRODUCT MANAGER
articles.summary: PMs often face silence and doubt after big wins, lacking quick validation. The MOVE Model helps create momentum and signals of progress when clarity is scarce.

articles.title: ROADMAP: DEVELOPER TOOLING FOR SOFTWARE 3.0
articles.summary: Software 3.0 is here: natural language is becoming the new programming interface, and AI agents are evolving from helpers to autonomous developers. This transformation is reshaping productivity, democratizing who can build, and creating new infrastructure and standards for the next generation of software.

articles.title: RUNNING A CLEAN REORG
articles.summary: Reorgs can feel scary, but when done well, they clarify priorities, empower teams, and fuel long-term growth. The key is to approach them with intention: make clear tradeoffs, communicate thoughtfully, move quickly, and treat them like a product launch.

articles.title: HOW TO DEFINE SUCCESS CRITERIA FOR UX RESEARCH
articles.summary: Strong UX research is built on hypotheses that frame assumptions about user behavior and success criteria that define measurable validation. Incorporating behavioral, attitudinal, and business-facing metrics ensures that research effectively drives both design and business outcomes.

articles.title: DISPOSABLE SOFTWARE
articles.summary: AI and cheap tools enable disposable, personal apps, shifting software creation from ROI constraints to pure imagination.

articles.title: TREAT HEADCOUNT AS A COST
articles.summary: Real growth comes from clarity, tight feedback loops, and enabling small teams to execute effectively.
